decision: Extract pure KDL-enrichment module (src/enrich.rs) with no zellij-tile dep; cargo test builds on host target.
rationale: Functions verified pure str->str at src/main.rs:65-263; whole point is host-testability.

decision: Crate stays a binary crate; never cdylib (cdylib emits no _start, Zellij loader rejects it).
rationale: register_plugin! generates fn main() for the _start export; locked invariant per context/architecture.md.

decision: KDL parsing stays v1 (parse_v1/ensure_v1); default v2 parse() rejected (fails on Zellij dumps).
rationale: Locked invariant; v2 parse() fails on Zellij's v1 dump.

decision: Resume uses --resume; --session-id is treated only as already-pinned, never injected to resume.
rationale: --session-id only assigns id to NEW session and errors on existing UUID.

decision: Behavior parity: extracted enrich_claude_panes produces byte-identical output for identical input (resolver held constant).
rationale: This is a refactor-for-testability, not a behavior change; parity is the acceptance bar.

decision: Tests live inline as #[cfg(test)] mod tests in src/enrich.rs (A3); marker resolver injected/stubbed (A7).
rationale: Idiomatic Rust; keeps fixtures next to code; injected resolver keeps module pure & hermetic. Exact seam is an S3 decision.

decision: neutralize_snap_pane strips command, args, AND start_suspended/close_on_exit CHILD nodes.
rationale: Jun-29 regression: leaving start_suspended on a command-less pane makes restore fail.

decision: Idempotent enrichment: pane already carrying --resume/--session-id is not double-injected.
rationale: pane_has_session_id treats either flag as pinned; re-running snap must be stable.

decision: Template subtrees (new_tab_template, tab_template, swap_tiled_layout, swap_floating_layout) skipped during enrichment.
rationale: They describe what to spawn for a NEW tab; pinning to an old session is wrong.

decision: resolve_cwd: relative pane cwd joined onto layout base (single trailing slash trimmed); absolute passes through; no-cwd inherits base; no cwd+no base -> None -> pane left bare.
rationale: Matches src/main.rs:210-216 behavior; encodes the four resolution branches.

decision: New push/PR CI workflow (separate from release.yml) runs cargo build --release --target wasm32-wasip1 + fmt --check + clippy -D warnings + cargo test; each gates the job.
rationale: No automated CI test job exists yet; release.yml is v*-tag only and stays unchanged.

decision: No coverage gate added to CI (A4); R-COV-001 scoped to extracted module only, host code excluded.
rationale: context/stack.md: coverage 'cannot be enforced here'; no coverage tool configured. Adding one is a scope addition needing sign-off.

decision: Runtime behavior MUST be byte-identical to pre-extraction; save flow stays synchronous (no async CustomMessage).
rationale: Locked invariants; async CustomMessage pattern silently saved nothing in a prior version.

decision: Model the change as a single capability `kdl-enrichment-testability` (one spec).
reasoning: The three deliverables (extract pure module, regression suite, push/PR CI) form one cohesive testability capability sharing the parity contract and Cargo.toml/crate-type invariants; one capability keeps the contract atomic.
rejected: Split into separate `module-extraction` + `ci-pipeline` specs — would fragment the shared parity contract and crate-type invariants.

decision: Test target = the PURE subset only (enrich_claude_panes, enrich_nodes, neutralize_snap_pane, resolve_cwd, pane_has_session_id, inject_session_id, basename, is_template_node).
reasoning: These are string-in/string-out, verified src/main.rs:65-263, so they build and test on the host target; pipe/load/dump/save/real marker read need the WASI host and stay manual/headless-PTY (Non-Goals).
rejected: Testing the trait methods / host calls — impossible on the host target without Zellij.

decision: Marker lookup is injected/stubbed in tests, not the real /tmp filesystem read.
reasoning: maybe_enrich_pane->resolve_session_uuid does std::fs::read_to_string; to keep enrich.rs pure and host-buildable the resolver must be a parameter/trait the tests supply. Exact mechanism (fn param vs trait vs cfg) deferred to S3.
rejected: Writing real marker files in /tmp during tests — non-hermetic, defeats the zellij-tile-free goal.

decision: Parity is the acceptance bar, expressed as byte-identical enrich_claude_panes output for identical input with the resolver held constant.
reasoning: This is a refactor-for-testability, NOT a behavior change; byte-identical output is the only safe pass condition.
rejected: Allowing semantic-equivalent-but-reformatted output — would mask serialization drift that breaks Zellij KDL v1 restore.

decision: CI = 4 commands (build wasm32-wasip1 + fmt --check + clippy -D warnings + cargo test), NO coverage gate.
reasoning: context/stack.md states R-COV-001 (>=80%) cannot be enforced here and no coverage tool is configured; release.yml stays v*-tag only and unchanged.
rejected: Adding tarpaulin/llvm-cov coverage gate — scope addition needing SPEC LOCK sign-off (A4).

decision: Marker-lookup seam = borrowed closure parameter (&dyn Fn(&str)->Option<String>) threaded through enrich_claude_panes->enrich_nodes->maybe_enrich_pane. main.rs passes &|cwd| resolve_session_uuid(cwd); tests pass an inline stub.
rationale: Smallest change keeping enrich.rs provably free of std::fs and zellij_tile (BR-tests-001, INT-tests-003, A7); same Option<String> contract as current code so output is byte-identical for a fixed resolver (AC-tests-007). Rejected trait (over-engineered for one lookup) and cfg-swap (keeps fs coupling in enrich.rs, cannot validate prod path).

decision: Test layout = inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests in src/enrich.rs (not a tests/ dir).
rationale: BR-tests-008 names internal helpers (neutralize_snap_pane, pane_has_session_id, is_template_node, resolve_cwd) that are pub(crate) — only reachable from an in-crate test module; a tests/ dir would force them pub, widening the API for no runtime reason. Inline keeps fixtures beside the parity contract.

decision: CI = new .github/workflows/ci.yml, on:[push,pull_request], single ubuntu-latest job, sequential fail-fast steps: install Rust+wasm32-wasip1 (mirror release.yml) -> cargo build --release --target wasm32-wasip1 -> cargo fmt --check -> cargo clippy -- -D warnings -> cargo test. NO coverage gate.
rationale: Mirrors release.yml runner/target-install; each step gates the job (AC-tests-027/028, BR-tests-009). A4: no coverage tool configured, R-COV-001 cannot be enforced here; coverage gate is a separate scoped change. release.yml untouched.

decision: Parity-bootstrap mitigation: extraction is a VERBATIM move of the 8 function bodies (only resolver-param + doc-comment additions); fixture expected outputs seeded from documented CURRENT behavior (spec scenarios + existing src/main.rs), not post-refactor output; ONE mandatory manual headless-PTY round-trip at S4 before parity sign-off.
rationale: Defends AC-tests-007 against both code drift and fixture drift (the HIGH parity-bootstrap risk has no prior test net). The PTY round-trip is already in the project verification playbook (context/stack.md).

decision: Logging stays at the I/O boundary: drop per-pane eprintln! from the pure walk; keep ONLY the parse-failure eprintln! in enrich_claude_panes; main.rs resolver closure may log on its own.
rationale: Pure functions stay deterministic and side-effect-light for clean test output; parse-failure breadcrumb (std-only, not a host call) is the documented graceful-degradation signal (BR-tests-007). Diagnostic-only change; returned KDL string unchanged so parity holds (AC-tests-007).

decision: openapi.yaml = N/A and DB migrations = N/A for this change.
rationale: No HTTP API (R-API-003 applies only to endpoints; none exist per context/conventions.md) and no database (R-DB-001 N/A); state is plain files. Recorded explicitly so the gate does not expect these artifacts.

decision: Extracted 8 functions verbatim into src/enrich.rs; added SessionResolver<'a> = dyn Fn(&str)->Option<String> + 'a closure seam; threaded resolve through enrich_claude_panes->enrich_nodes->maybe_enrich_pane; dropped per-pane eprintln! from pure walk (ADR-005); fixed stale --session-id doc-comment.
rationale: Verbatim move (ADR-004) preserves parity; closure seam (ADR-001) keeps enrich.rs provably free of std::fs and zellij_tile; diagnostic cleanup (ADR-005) keeps test output clean.

decision: Added #[cfg(not(test))] guards in src/main.rs around: use zellij_tile::prelude::*, use std::collections::BTreeMap, struct State, impl ZellijPlugin for State, register_plugin!(State). Added #[cfg_attr(test, allow(dead_code))] to resolve_session_uuid.
rationale: Without these guards, cargo test on the native host fails at link time: zellij_tile references host_run_plugin_command (a WASI-only symbol) which the native linker cannot resolve. This is the standard Rust idiom for binary crates with WASM-only host dependencies. Zero runtime or WASM behavior change (cfg(test) is never set for wasm32-wasip1 target). WASM build passes; binary crate invariant preserved.

decision: enrich_nodes signature changed from &mut Vec<KdlNode> to &mut [KdlNode] to satisfy clippy::ptr_arg (-D warnings). The function only iterates the slice; callers pass Vec::nodes_mut() which auto-coerces to &mut [KdlNode] via Deref.
rationale: cargo clippy -- -D warnings is a CI gate (AC-tests-028); clippy::ptr_arg fires on &mut Vec when a slice suffices. The change is correct and safe (no push/remove at this level). Logged as a minor implementation detail, not a design deviation.

decision: Added 33-test #[cfg(test)] mod tests inline in src/enrich.rs covering all 28 ACs + 10 BRs + 4 INTs. Placeholder UUIDs only (00000000-... and 11111111-...). resolver_some/resolver_none stub closures. All test names include their AC-ID.
rationale: ADR-002 (inline tests for pub(crate) access); ADR-004 (fixtures seeded from current documented behavior); R-SEC-001 (no real session IDs in fixtures).

decision: MINOR — src/main.rs required #[cfg(not(test))] guards (see IMPL-002). ADR-002 stated inline tests in a binary crate run via cargo test without any crate-type change, which is correct, but did not address the WASI host-symbol linker issue.
rationale: The guards are the idiomatic Rust solution; no design change required. WASM build, parity, and binary-crate invariant all unaffected.
impact: None on runtime behavior or parity. main.rs is slightly more verbose. The cfg(not(test)) pattern is transparent to the WASM target.

decision: parity_claude_pane_enriched_with_resume_ac_tests_007 uses contains() substring checks rather than exact byte-identical output comparison; empty/whitespace no-panic tests use `let _ = result` with no assertions. AC-tests-007 spec requires byte-identical output but the test only verifies key substrings are present.
rca: S4 implementation choice — not a design or spec gap. Idempotency test (assert_eq! first==second) + human PTY round-trip provide sufficient practical coverage. Advisory: follow-on change should add exact-output fixture.
impact: Low: bounded by pinned KDL crate version; idempotency test + PTY PASS provide compensating coverage. Not a blocker.

decision: F-001 resolved: replaced 3 weak assertions with exact-output checks. (1) parity_claude_pane_enriched_with_resume_ac_tests_007: replaced 3x contains() calls with assert_eq!(result, PARITY_EXPECTED) against a const byte-identical expected string derived from the fixture + injection rule (KDL v1 serializer escapes / as \/, emits injected args without leading indent, terminates with ;). (2) empty_input_no_panic_ac_tests_009: replaced let _ = result with assert_eq!(result, ""). (3) whitespace_only_input_no_panic_ac_tests_009: replaced let _ = result with assert_eq!(result, "   \n\t  "). All 33 tests still pass; fmt --check PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS.
rationale: AC-tests-007 / BR-tests-002 require byte-identical output comparison; contains() only verified key substrings were present. Empty/whitespace tests with no assertion would not catch a behavioral regression on those paths.

decision: F-001 retest confirmed CLOSED. PARITY_EXPECTED is non-trivially distinct from PARITY_INPUT (contains escaped cwd \/home\/user and injected args "--resume" "00000000-..." child node); assert_eq! genuinely verifies enrichment occurred and serializer output is exact. Empty test asserts result==""; whitespace test asserts result=="   \n\t  ". cargo test 33/33 PASS; fmt --check PASS; clippy -D warnings PASS. No production code changed.

S6 release artifacts produced: release.md written to openspec/changes/add-unit-tests/release.md covering release notes (28 AC-IDs, 4 regression areas), migration checklist (DB N/A, no dep changes, docs-sync advisory), rollback plan (git revert; clean — 3 files, release.yml untouched), post-deploy smoke test (cache purge + fresh session required), deploy strategy (PR merge to main; optional v0.2.0 tag for release.yml). Commit message per R-GIT-001: feat(enrich): extract pure KDL module + regression tests + push/PR CI. Change archived via openspec archive.
rationale: S5 GO with 0 Critical/High bugs, F-001 CLOSED, parity human-confirmed. S6 completes the SDLC pipeline for add-unit-tests.
